name: Sushi Ginza Onodera
tagline: Tokyo-level omakase in Waikiki — the fish is flown in from Toyosu and the craft is impeccable.
mustOrder: Omakase — the full chef's counter experience, ~20 pieces of seasonal nigiri
Otoro — fatty tuna belly, melt-in-your-mouth perfection
Uni — seasonal sea urchin, often from Hokkaido
parking: Valet at the hotel or public parking at Waikiki Trade Center garage on Kuhio Ave.
talkStory: Part of a global omakase empire founded by Koji Onodera with flagship locations in Ginza, New York, and LA. The Honolulu outpost opened in the Trump International Waikiki and flies fish directly from Tokyo's Toyosu Market. Omakase runs $200-300+ per person — a genuine splurge, but the craft rivals top sushi-ya in Japan. Sit at the counter for the full itamae experience. Reservations essential, especially weekends.
